prefix_anonymized: Love how some even double as art galleries!
Cafés = spontaneous teacher meetings & student study sessions!
New spots can be great places for informal gatherings if they stick around long enough - endless opportunities for group activities or book clubs!
Also perfect for impromptu staff meetings!
Such a shame when unique spots get overrun by generic chains... even our treasured old gathering spot got swapped for yet another retail store!
Miss old bookstore corner too - now just another sterile mini-mart.
Charm isn't replicable; people forget that.
Gotta admit old landmarks had their charm; worrying though if families can keep up with those hikes in living costs...
Magic beans are one thing but ever heard someone pouring money into 'educational' games claiming they're legit for learning? Talk about fantasy investments!
Noticed similar trends - inclusivity's up within our teams too!

Miss old bookstore corner too - now just another sterile mini-mart.
Charm isn't replicable; people forget that.
Gotta admit old landmarks had their charm; worrying though if families can keep up with those hikes in living costs...
Magic beans are one thing but ever heard someone pouring money into 'educational' games claiming they're legit for learning? Talk about fantasy investments!
Noticed similar trends - inclusivity's up within our teams too!
Schools today champion skill over stereotype!
In schools now seeing boys knitting & girls coding - progress!
Teachers actively challenge these old-school norms nowadays yet some outdated views stick like gum under desks—incredibly stubborn!
Seeing more girls into STEM subjects at school nowadays!
Both aspects can catch you off-guard tbh!
Big spaces mask quaint old-world charm often overlooked at first glance!

While nostalgia's great and all, let's not forget practical stuff like access to quality education matter too. That probably had a bigger impact on career paths way more than just tales from old times did.
